reasoning: User asked specifically for hanabi (fireworks) festivals. The tool returned 312 intangible folk cultural properties — none of which are fireworks events. All results are shrine festivals, performing arts, and ritual ceremonies. The tool call is a broad get_festivals with no keyword filter, so the corpus does not surface any 花火大会 (fireworks festivals), which are not part of the intangible heritage data layer. Completely misses the intent.
missing_data: Actual hanabi taikai (fireworks festivals) such as Sumida River Fireworks, Nagaoka Fireworks, Omagari; seasonal dates; viewing tips. The corpus may lack fireworks-specific data.
improvement_hint: Add a fireworks-category tag or dedicated 花火大会 dataset. Alternatively, search_area with '花火' keyword would surface municipal scrape content mentioning fireworks events.

reasoning: Same issue as L3-01 but in Japanese. User wants to see fireworks; response returns 312 intangible folk cultural heritage items, none are hanabi taikai. The ja language param is correctly handled (name/description fields show Japanese text). But topical relevance is entirely absent — the tool call cannot surface fireworks events from this data source.
missing_data: Fireworks festival listings with dates, locations, scale (number of shells), viewing access info.
improvement_hint: Use search_area with q='花火' to pull municipal DMO content, or add a hanabi sub-category to get_festivals. Flower fireworks events are distinct from intangible cultural properties.

reasoning: User wants snow festival experiences in Japan; Hokkaido is the natural target. The response returns 0 prefecture-specific items and falls back to national_heritage items (Dainichido Bugaku from Akita, Hayachine Kagura from Iwate, Kawagoe Hikawa festival from Saitama, etc.) — none in Hokkaido, none snow-related. The Hokkaido filter returned empty and the fallback national items are entirely off-topic. Sapporo Snow Festival and Asahikawa Winter Festival are the obvious answers but completely absent.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, Otaru Snow Light Path; Hokkaido winter event data; snow festival dates (late Jan–Feb).
wrong_data: National heritage fallback includes festivals in Saitama, Mie, Kanagawa — not relevant to Hokkaido snow festivals at all.
improvement_hint: Add snow festival data (yukimatsuri) from Hokkaido DMO sources. The Hokkaido festival corpus appears empty; investigate why and scrape Sapporo and Asahikawa DMO pages.

reasoning: User wants to experience rural life (田舎暮らし体験) in Japan. The tool returns Japan Heritage stories in Chinese — content is well-translated but none of the items address rural living experiences. First result is about ancient Asuka women, second is about Kamakura. These are heritage stories, not rural experience opportunities. The intent requires satoyama stays, farm tours, kominka lodging — none appear.
missing_data: Satoyama experience programs, farm stays (農家民宿), traditional rural village listings, kominka lodging with rural lifestyle activities.
improvement_hint: Add a 'rural experience' category to the specialty/experience data. get_japan_heritage without a theme filter returns unranked results; add semantic filtering for '農村' or '田舎' themed stories.

reasoning: User wants autumn leaf (momiji/koyo) viewing recommendations in Korean. The search returns 81 hits, many genuinely named 紅葉 (momiji). Top results include Momijidani Park in Hiroshima (Miyajima — excellent koyo spot), various momiji-named parks, Sakurajima (off-topic), historic sites. Result set contains legitimate leaf-viewing locations though ranking mixes in weakly relevant items. Heritage designations and prominence scores present. Korean lang param not surfaced in response names but that is expected — names remain in ja/en. Acceptable output for an agent to synthesize.
missing_data: Korean-translated names for main results; best timing information for koyo season; iconic spots like Nikko, Kyoto (Eikan-do) not surfaced because query is by name rather than attribute.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal attribute tagging (koyo_spot=true) so dedicated autumn-leaf queries can retrieve canonically famous sites rather than relying on name-matching '紅葉'.

reasoning: User wants a quiet non-touristy meditation temple. Query '修行' (spiritual training/ascetic practice) returns 75 hits. Top results include Shugyo-ji Temple in Tokyo (useful), STAY YAMAGATA tourism portal pages (irrelevant noise), Manjushuin (Kyoto — heritage), Zuishinin (Kyoto). Municipal scrape spots are generic tourism portals with no meditation relevance. The YAMAGATA spots are pure nav-chrome. Wikidata attractions with heritage designations are more useful but buried behind noise. The 'not touristy' constraint is impossible to address without visitor-crowd data.
missing_data: Zazen experience programs, temple stay (shukubo) listings, specific retreat centers like Eiheiji, Koyasan; opening hours; access info.
wrong_data: Manjushuin description_en says 'anglican priest' — clearly wrong/placeholder Wikidata entry.
improvement_hint: Filter out municipal_scrape spots that are pure navigation pages (cookie-only descriptions). Add shukubo/temple-stay as a lodging sub-category.

reasoning: User asks about aurora borealis in Japan (aurora are only marginally visible from northern Hokkaido during strong solar events — a very niche topic). The search returns 85 hits for 'オーロラ' but none are aurora-viewing spots. Results include Oita tourism business site, Iwate tourism feature page, Sanrio Puroland amusement park, Furano DMO plans, Ofunato DMO. Sanrio Puroland appearing as a top result for 'aurora' is a ranking failure. No useful aurora-watching information returned. The corpus simply lacks aurora data.
missing_data: Hokkaido aurora viewing info (Rishiri, Wakkanai), solar activity dependency note, seasonal timing (winter, high solar activity), any legitimate aurora spot.
wrong_data: Sanrio Puroland has no aurora relevance — ranking failure.
improvement_hint: Flag impossible/near-impossible queries (aurora viewing in Japan is extremely rare). The agent should explain rarity. Add 'aurora' as a handled edge-case with appropriate caveat text rather than returning unrelated hits.

reasoning: User wants to sleep in a 100-year-old house (kominka). Tool retrieves Gifu hotels — a reasonable prefecture choice given Takayama/Shirakawa-go area. Results include Airbnb Omotenashi Teru House, AOI SUITE TAKAYAMA (hostel), Oyado koto no yume, Hotel Hotaka, Route Inn (business hotel), Okuhida Garden Hotel. None are identified as kominka or historic buildings. The list is just a generic hotel catalog with OSM sources — no age/style metadata. Cannot distinguish 100-year-old houses from modern business hotels.
missing_data: Kominka designation, building age, traditional style flags; Shirakawa-go gassho-style lodging; Takayama machiya stays; booking links.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_style or heritage_building field to hotel records. Tag accommodations that are kominka/machiya/gassho-zukuri based on OSM/wikidata heritage attributes.

reasoning: User (Thai) wants hidden/non-touristy cherry blossom spots. Query '桜' returns 161 hits. Top results include Sakurada-mon gate (very touristy Imperial Palace area), Sakuragi Station (heritage railway station — not a sakura spot), Sakurai Shrine (not a sakura spot), Cherry and Maple Trees artwork object. Most results match '桜' by name only, not because they are cherry blossom viewing spots. The 'non-touristy' constraint is unaddressable. Lang=th but Thai translations absent. Good data volume but poor topical relevance for the actual need.
missing_data: Hidden/lesser-known cherry blossom spots; blooming season data; crowd level indicators; Thai-translated names.
improvement_hint: Add sakura_spot attribute and crowd_level metadata. Name-matching '桜' retrieves administrative/heritage items named after cherry blossoms, not viewing spots. A dedicated cherry blossom POI layer would help greatly.

reasoning: Photographer wants dramatic landscapes. Query '絶景' (spectacular scenery) returns 51 hits. Top results include an Iwate photo download page, Niigata scenic spots page (with real content about Japan Sea coast, sunflower fields, cliffs), Ehime scenic drives with 13 specific named spots (good specificity). Municipal scrape results have substantive description content this time. Less noise than other cases, though the STAY YAMAGATA entry is somewhat irrelevant. Niigata and Ehime scenic content genuinely helps an agent. Could be much richer but acceptable baseline.
missing_data: Famous dramatic landscape icons like Mt. Fuji, Tottori Sand Dunes, Nachi Falls, Okinawa sea; coordinates for most scrape spots; photography access times.
improvement_hint: Add a scenery/photography category with curated top-tier 絶景 locations. Current results rely heavily on DMO article text rather than structured spot data.

reasoning: User (Arabic) wants outdoor hot springs in nature, not hotels. Query '露天風呂' (open-air bath) returns 73 hits. Results include Iwate onsen facility list (with Sukawa Onsen at 1,125m altitude, natural strong acid sulfur spring — exactly what user wants), Hosenbo Onsen with star-gazing outdoor bath, Yamaguchi scenery outdoor bath article. Content quality is good — descriptions are detailed and in Japanese but an agent can process them. The 'not hotels' constraint isn't perfectly addressed but outdoor/nature context is present. One of the better responses in this batch.
missing_data: Arabic-translated content; wild/riverside rotenburo like Takaragawa or Hoheikyo; mixed bathing (konyoku) options; accessibility notes.
improvement_hint: Add outdoor_type=riverside/mountain/cliff field to onsen data to help agents distinguish natural outdoor baths from hotel rotenburo gardens.

reasoning: User wants to swim with wild dolphins. Query 'イルカ' returns 62 hits. Top result is Kyoto tourism ambassador page (irrelevant). Second is Noto-jima Aquarium in Ishikawa (aquarium dolphin show — not wild swimming). Third is Teradomari fish market (fish market, not dolphin related). The aquarium result is partially relevant but contradicts the 'wild' constraint. Mikurajima (Tokyo) and Amitori Bay (Kochi) where wild dolphin swimming tours actually exist are absent. The corpus lacks ecotourism/wildlife tour data.
missing_data: Mikurajima dolphin swimming tours, Ogasawara wild dolphin encounters, Amitori Bay (Kochi), booking season (May-Oct typically), permit/guide requirements.
improvement_hint: Add ecotourism/wildlife experience category. Wild dolphin swimming is a niche but real product in Japan; dedicated municipal pages for Mikurajima would surface if scraped.

reasoning: User wants traditional architecture preserved from modernization (重伝建 districts). The tool returns Japan Heritage stories in English — good translation quality. But content is thematic heritage stories (Asuka women, Kamakura), not listings of traditional building preservation districts (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区). The corpus has heritage stories but not the specific traditional townscape/architecture districts the user needs. Shirakawa-go, Gion, Tsumago, Uchiko etc. are absent.
missing_data: Judenken (重伝建) district listings: Shirakawa-go, Tsumago-juku, Uchiko, Gion Shimbashi, Ouchi-juku; preservation status details; visiting access.
improvement_hint: Add 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 (Important Preservation Districts for Groups of Traditional Buildings) as a dedicated data layer — these are the precise answer to this query type.

reasoning: User (Vietnamese) wants to experience Ainu culture in Hokkaido. Query 'アイヌ' returns 91 hits. Results are highly relevant: National Ainu Museum (Upopoy) in Shiraoi, Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum in Biratori, Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum in Asahikawa, Teshikaga Kussharo Kotan Ainu Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center in Sapporo, Sapporo Ainu Culture Promotion Center — all in Hokkaido prefecture 01. Also includes the cultural landscape designation for Sarugawa River. This is an excellent result set with coordinates for major museums. Best response in this batch.
missing_data: Vietnamese-translated museum names; specific programs (craft workshops, Upopoy performance schedules); admission fees; access from Sapporo.
wrong_data: heritage_designations_labels for Sarugawa River cultural landscape shows Q64576748 (unresolved QID) instead of a human-readable label.
improvement_hint: Resolve all heritage_designations_labels from QID to human-readable strings. The Sarugawa landscape label is still a raw QID. Add experience program data for Upopoy.

reasoning: User wants cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Query returns 73 hits. Top hits are precisely on target: Usuki Stone Buddhas (Oita, National Treasure + Special Historic Site), Oya-dera magaibutsu (Tochigi, Important Cultural Property + Special Historic Site), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima), plus several smaller examples. All properly designated with multiple heritage labels. Coordinates provided for major sites. This is a niche query answered with authoritative designated-heritage data. Very strong retrieval performance.
missing_data: Visiting hours, admission fees, access from nearest major city; Kunisaki Peninsula magaibutsu cluster (multiple sites in Oita not individually listed).
wrong_data: Q3076940 Manjushuin described as 'anglican priest' — clearly wrong description_en (Wikidata data quality issue), though it appears in a different case context.
improvement_hint: Ensure description_en fields are validated — some Wikidata entries have clearly incorrect descriptions. Add access/transport info for remote magaibutsu sites.

reasoning: User wants day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. Query '火山' returns 56 hits. Top result is 火山 (Hiyama) mountain in Fukuoka with no coordinates — irrelevant to Tokyo. Then several Kumamoto/Aso volcano pages (in Chinese, 700km from Tokyo). The obvious answers (Mt. Fuji, Hakone/Mt. Kamiyama, Izu-Oshima, Mt. Tsukuba) are absent. The 'day trip from Tokyo' constraint is completely ignored — results cluster in Kyushu. Aso is 800km from Tokyo, not day-trip feasible. Travel feasibility is poor.
missing_data: Hakone volcano hiking (Mt. Kamiyama, Owakudani), Izu-Oshima, Mt. Fuji (Yoshida Trail), Nikko-Shirane — all accessible from Tokyo within a day.
wrong_data: Aso presented in results but is not day-trip feasible from Tokyo.
improvement_hint: Add geographic proximity filtering capability (distance from major hub cities). Without it, the query '火山' pulls Kyushu content rather than Kanto/proximity results.

reasoning: User (Indonesian) wants to eat freshly made tofu at a temple. Query '豆腐' returns 93 hits. Results include Fukui tourism language selector page, Iwate ryokan page, Kumamoto food guide, cookie-consent-only pages, and eventually an Iwayama-frozen-tofu GI designation. None address the specific experience of eating freshly made temple tofu. Famous options like Nanzenji area tofu restaurants, Eihei-ji temple food, or Koyasan shojin ryori are absent. The 'temple' constraint is completely unaddressed.
missing_data: Nanzenji tofu restaurants (Kyoto), shojin ryori at Koyasan/Eihei-ji, temple-made yudofu experiences, tofu-making workshop listings.
improvement_hint: Add experience/activity tagging for food workshops. Shojin ryori and temple tofu is a well-known Japan travel product that should appear in DMO content — check if Kyoto/Fukui/Nara pages are scraped with this content.

reasoning: User wants fishing villages frozen in time. Query '漁港' returns 84 hits. Results include Teradomari fish market (Niigata — active working fish market, decent), Kitakyushu sea tourism page (nav-chrome only), Kesennuma fishing port DMO page (good — describes active port, seafood, culture, post-3/11 recovery story), Notsuke Bay (Hokkaido, Ramsar wetland), Shimane cat/fishing promo. Kesennuma content is substantive and paints a picture of a working fishing town. The 'frozen in time' (historic preservation) aspect is weak — most ports shown are active, not preserved villages like Ine no Funaya.
missing_data: Ine Funaya (Kyoto — traditional boat-house fishing village, most on-target answer), Hegura Island, Sado Island fishing settlements; historic preservation context.
improvement_hint: Add 'funaya' (boat-house traditional architecture) as a category. Ine town in Kyoto is the canonical answer to this query type and should be in the corpus.

reasoning: User wants to learn traditional paper-making (washi) from a master. Tool uses get_local_specialty(category='craft') returning 231 METI traditional craft items. First results are Nibutani Attus (Ainu fabric — not paper), Oitama Tsumugi (silk textile — not paper), Ugo Shina cloth (bark fiber cloth — adjacent but not washi). Response doesn't surface washi specifically because craft list starts from item 0101 and washi items (Echizen, Mino, Ogawa/Hosokawa washi) are elsewhere in the list. Data quality of returned items is excellent with full descriptions, production areas, designation dates. But washi-specific items are not in the visible truncated results.
missing_data: Washi (和紙) entries specifically: Echizen Washi, Mino Washi, Ogawa Washi, Hosokawa Washi; experience/workshop programs at paper-making studios.
improvement_hint: Add keyword search parameter to get_local_specialty so agents can filter by craft type (e.g., q='和紙'). The flat list starting from index 0101 means washi items may be buried deep in the 231-item result set.

reasoning: User wants period-drama-style scenery (宿場町 — post-town townscapes). Query '宿場' returns 90 hits. Top results are excellent: Nagano DMO article about Suharajuku and Nojiriku on Nakasendo with detailed description of water-boats, historic architecture; Ueda's Yanagimachi post town (300m historic street, sake brewery, machiya cafes, wheelchair accessible); Narai-juku in Nagano (Important Preservation District designation confirmed); Kyu-Nateshu Honjin in Wakayama (ICP). These are exactly the right answers. Prominence scores and heritage designations present. One of the strongest responses in this batch.
missing_data: Tsumago/Magome (classic Nakasendo post towns), Ouchi-juku (Fukushima), photography permits/guidance; seasonal access information.
wrong_data: Akasaka Guesthouse (迎賓館赤坂離宮) appearing in results is unrelated to post-towns — it's a state guesthouse.
improvement_hint: The Akasaka Guesthouse result shows that '宿場' query pulls any item with '宿' in the name. Better semantic filtering would reduce such noise.

reasoning: User wants uncrowded beaches. Query 'ビーチ' returns 81 hits. Results include named beaches: Naminoue Beach (Okinawa city — actually crowded), Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach (Hyogo), Sunayama Beach (Miyako-jima), Tropical Beach (Okinawa), Painagama Beach (Miyako-jima), Yonehara Beach (Ishigaki). The Miyako/Ishigaki beaches are genuinely less crowded relative to Okinawa main island. Coordinates present. The 'not crowded' constraint cannot be addressed without visitor data, but the set includes some genuinely lesser-known options. Minami Chita Beach Land is an amusement park not a beach.
missing_data: Crowd level data; remote island beaches (Ogasawara, Amami, Yoron); seasonal timing; ferry access information for island beaches.
wrong_data: Minami Chita Beach Land is a marine park/amusement complex, not a natural uncrowded beach.
improvement_hint: Add beach_type=natural/artificial and remoteness_tier fields. Filter amusement parks from beach results. Include remote island beach coverage from Amami, Ogasawara DMO sources.

reasoning: User wants a small old-school izakaya yokocho (alley) to drink with locals. Query '横丁' returns 80 hits. Top results include Yokocho Park (park in Tokyo — irrelevant), Saga tourism page (about nature/ceramics, not izakaya), 横被 (a fabric artifact in Kyoto with National Treasure designation — completely unrelated), a dessert yokocho, Haaikara yokocho amusement park in Yamaguchi. Actual izakaya alley content like Omoide Yokocho, Shinjuku Golden Gai, Osaka Hozenji Yokocho, Sapporo Susukino are absent. Results are dominated by name-matching noise.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Shinjuku), Golden Gai, Nonbei Yokocho (Shibuya), Ebisu Yokocho, Osaka Hozenji Yokocho, Sapporo Tanuki Koji; izakaya atmosphere descriptions.
wrong_data: 横被 (a ceremonial robe artifact) has nothing to do with izakaya alleys — pure name-matching artifact.
improvement_hint: Differentiate '横丁' (alley/food district) from items merely containing '横' in name. Add izakaya/bar district as a place category in municipal scrape data.

reasoning: User (German) wants to travel by rural train through Japan. Query 'ローカル線' returns 61 hits. Top result is excellent Nagano DMO article about Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line (11.6km, 15 stations, red bridge over Chikuma River, rural scenery, hot spring terminus) with very specific timetable/frequency info. Second result is a Kanto model course using Kashima Rinkai Railway with actual itinerary including Hitachi Seaside Park, Oarai. Third result describes Amami access info. Fourth result is Tsugaru DMO plan mentioning two local railways. Strong, specific content for this intent.
missing_data: German-translated content; Iya Valley ropeway, Akechi Railway, Gono Line (Akita-Aomori coastline) — iconic scenic routes not surfaced; JR Pass compatibility notes.
improvement_hint: The Nagano and Ibaraki DMO content is high quality. Ensure more prefectures' scenic railway articles are scraped — Akita Naisen Line, Sanriku Railway, Shimane Ichibata Densha would add variety.

reasoning: User wants firefly viewing spots in summer. Query 'ホタル' returns 72 hits. Top results include: Hotaru-no-Sato park (Hyogo), Firefly Museum of Toyota Town (Yamaguchi), Kitakyushu firefly viewing page, Yamaguchi tourism page with detailed Hotaru-bune river cruise (6/5-6/21, 800m ride, adult ¥2,000, descriptions of Genji firefly habitat as national natural monument), Nagasaki firefly guide with viewing etiquette (8-9pm best time, avoid lights, don't touch). Excellent substantive content with specific dates, prices, and tips. One of the strongest responses in the batch.
missing_data: Motosu Lake, Tatsuno City (Hyogo), Okutama (Tokyo) — other well-known firefly spots; accommodation near firefly sites; bloom/season year-to-year variability note.
improvement_hint: The firefly content is strong. Add coordinates to municipal_scrape spots so agents can show maps. The Nagasaki and Yamaguchi content quality is a model for what good DMO scraping looks like.

reasoning: User (Russian) wants to see cranes wintering in Japan. Query '鶴' returns 161 hits. Top results: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu shrine (a shrine named '鶴', not a crane habitat), Kakurinji Temple halls (temple named '鶴', not crane related), Tsuruma Park in Nagoya (park named '鶴'). All top results match '鶴' as a name component, not as the bird. Izumi City in Kagoshima (where 10,000+ hooded cranes winter) and Tsurui Village in Hokkaido (red-crowned crane habitat) are entirely absent. Pure name-matching failure for a wildlife query.
missing_data: Izumi City crane wintering grounds (Kagoshima, 10,000+ cranes), Tsurui-Ito Tancho Sanctuary (Hokkaido), Kushiro Wetlands crane habitat, viewing season (Nov-Mar), organized tour options.
improvement_hint: Add wildlife/nature observation as a category with specific fauna tags (tancho=red-crowned crane, etc.). Name-based search for '鶴' is fundamentally unsuitable for wildlife location queries — requires semantic/conceptual matching.
